methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $4.0B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Colossal Biosciences, a Series C-stage biotech company founded by Ben Lamm and George Church, is valued at $1.5B post-money following $435M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies in Biotech & Health: 3 public-market anchors (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers at Series B–D stages, including Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Insitro ($2.4B), and HistoSonics ($2.2B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier from Series C to the next funding round, implies $2.5B. Peer-median analysis across 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields $1.8B, with a range spanning $600M to $4.0B. Public-market anchoring via P/S multiples (median 6.6x across three tickers) cannot be applied due to unavailable revenue data, typical for private companies.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.5B to $2.9B, with a median of $2.2B derived from the two applicable methods. The current $1.5B valuation aligns with the lower bound. Key uncertainties include the timing and execution risk of the next funding event, which drives the stage-progression multiple, and the company's relative progress on clinical and commercial milestones versus the peer set.
